# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Ships at Sea
- Steam AppID of the game: 1266540

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX 6700 XT
- Video driver version: Mesa 25.1.1
- Kernel version: 6.14.9-zen1
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/bjo81/9b2d3bf0afb42bcd6cec26d551c60d1f
- Proton version: proton-9.0-4e

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Ingame within the ships a radio could play radio streams.  These don't work.


## Reproduction

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ae61ee7e-c340-4c16-adfd-9378a9310d16)
Switch on the radio and press "source". Streams like "Simulator Radio" appear, it seems it tries to connect but fails.

[steam-1266540.log.tar.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20532980/steam-1266540.log.tar.gz)

following_discussion.discussion_body:

40605.899:0130:01ac:warn:seh:OutputDebugStringW L"[2025.10.12-16.55.45:329][523]LogWmfMedia: Error: Failed to resolve URL https://ice2.somafm.com/groovesalad-128-aac: Unexpected error.\r\n"

issue_report_title: Automation - The Car Company Tycoon Game (293760)
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Automation - The Car Company Tycoon Game
- Steam AppID of the game: 293760

## System Information
- GPU: Radeon RX 6900 XT
- Driver/LLVM version: 4.6 Mesa 22.1.0-devel (git-a5fa7e0 2022-02-24 focal-oibaf-ppa)
- Kernel version: 5.15.6-051506-generic
- Link to full system information: https://gist.github.com/RoyvEmpel/a55110925f17b75ac24fdc1f07d5f96f
- Proton version: 7.0-1

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-293760.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/8150005/steam-293760.log)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Launching the game simply does nothing, adding PROTON_NO_D3D11 / PROTON_NO_D3D10 to the launch options starts the game launcher but that crashes / hangs after a few seconds.

The game it self does run when using the 'No Launcher' option but that makes it so you can't use workshop content. (the launcher enables them)

I looked at protonDB and tried using the windows 7 setting & installing dotnet 4.5.2 but that didn't work.

## Reproduction
1. Install automation on steam
2. Start the game
3. Select the first launch option
4. Press play
5. Game doesn't start

following_discussion.discussion_body:
I did some work earlier on ELYON, which wasn't enough to get it working, but that also used CefSharp so this report motivated me to get the existing work (mostly, ran into a problem with one of the changes) cleaned up and committed, and it turns out it's enough to allow this launcher to start.

So that should work with the build from this link, once the build process completes: https://github.com/madewokherd/wine-mono/actions/runs/1974181683

Instructions for installing custom wine-mono: https://github.com/redmcg/wine-mono/wiki#install-later-version

Hi @madewokherd! It seems like the build has succeeded but the linux-test has failed.
Can I still use the make output?
Sure, it just means I might have broken something, I'll have to check on that later.

Great, the launcher is working now :D
Thank you!

## I confirm:

- [Y ] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->
[steam-253230.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/15203847/steam-253230.log)


## Symptoms
these games work very well only first time i run them but it will freeze the whole system the second time i run them until i update the NVIDIA drivers or reinstall them but if i run only using intel gpu it will works well.

also the nvidia log:[nvidia-bug-report.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/15204042/nvidia-bug-report.log)

following_discussion.discussion_body: Hello @boxyld, this issue should also be mentioned to your video driver vendor.
oh forgot to add...it's dell laptop (Dell G15 5511)

I am experiencing a similar issue, but I'm not sure if this is the same issue, so I have a few more questions to clarify:

> will freeze the whole system the second time i run them

How do you recover from that freeze? Is the system completely unresponsive, or is it just very, very slow to respond? On my machine I can Alt-Tab out of the game - it takes about 30 seconds between pressing the keys and the switch to actually happen, but once I'm back to the desktop everything is fully responsive and I can close the game from the Steam client. Or I can press Alt-F4 inside the game and it will eventually close. Is the same true on your machine?

> until i update the NVIDIA drivers or reinstall them

What if you install a different Proton version? On my machine it does help *for a while*. I switched from Proton Experimental to 9, to 8, and now to 7. Every such switch makes the problem go away for a while, but it eventually re-appears. Also, going back to already "used" Proton version does not help.

Almost same as yours but...i could not even switch to tty

Changeing proton version is also help...or reinstall driver agin also help

following_discussion.discussion_body: Does native version has the same issue?

> Does native version has the same issue?

I don't encounter this issue in the feral-cat native version.

(I prefer to play on the windows-proton version because its performance much better than the native version)
I know this could potentially be old news but does anyone have issues with no mouse cursor even at the starting menu when using any proton version?  I'm using Manjaro XFCE.

Sadly does not start with Proton 9 or experimental, looking at the steam logs I only see this

I'm using an AMD RX 7800 xt card

```
 Setting breakpad minidump AppID = 779340
Steam_SetMinidumpSteamID:  Caching Steam ID:  76561198149193970 [API loaded no]
pid 96556 != 96555, skipping destruction (fork without exec?)
```

The native version sadly has some bugs with auto resolve and large campaigns, thats why I was trying proton.

With proton 8 it works

@raaffaaeell Hi there
Could you attach a full Proton log?

> @raaffaaeell Hi there Could you attach a full Proton log?

Here you go, I was going to work on it today

[steam-779340.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/14656728/steam-779340.log)

@raaffaaeell It turns out, the version of Proton GE you were using was behind current shipped Proton, and the regression you found is already fixed in both 9.0-beta and 9.0-experimental.  There was a further regression in experimental, but that is fixed in bleeding-edge.  Please let us know if you hit any further regressions (with mainline Proton) :)

@alasky17 you  are right, that was my bad, but in fairness, I tried all the Proton 9 versions I had, sorry for the confusion.

Bleeding edge indeed worked but I dont like using that one because of frequent updates.

The only thing I noticed, when in campaign, when accessing the menu (esc) the mouse lags a lot, but it seems to happen in version 8 too nowadays (I tested with the newest version there too)

Thanks for the quick response, I'll wait for the fix in the mainline versions

@raaffaaeell Thank you for confirming the fix!  Nice to know we don't have multiple issues floating about :). The fix for the crash further in is now in non-bleeding-edge experimental.  For what it is worth, the crash on launch is also fixed in 9.0-beta.  Right now both are updating ~1x week while we are in the beta period, which hopefully isn't too frequent to be usable :)

(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms

## Reproduction
Launch, go to check out, wait for customer with credit card, click on credit card machine, unable to input numbers with numpad.

following_discussion.discussion_body: https://steamcommunity.com/app/2670630/discussions/0/4289187621811000820/ [fandom.com/wiki](https://supermarket-simulator.fandom.com/wiki/FAQ#A_function_to_control_the_Credit_card_pad_via_the_Numpad_on_the_keyboard?) suggests this is a general game issue for some players.

> https://steamcommunity.com/app/2670630/discussions/0/4289187621811000820/ [fandom.com/wiki](https://supermarket-simulator.fandom.com/wiki/FAQ#A_function_to_control_the_Credit_card_pad_via_the_Numpad_on_the_keyboard?) suggests this is a general game issue for some players. I tested on my Windows 10 machine and it works, so this must have been fixed recently on Windows but that patch doesn't work under proton. Edit: It seems it can be worked around with the following keyboard option in the key bindings under KDE's layout options. Though it seems to happen with other desktop environments too. Enabling this makes the keypad work correctly. Another fix is also using `PROTON_ENABLE_WAYLAND=1` to enable Wayland windowing, which also solves the problem. It seems this is a bug perhaps within either the X11 implementation of WINE.

compatibility_report:
Age of Mythology Retold (1934680)

Issue transferred from https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/9110.
@patwre posted on 2025-10-17T05:59:40:

# Compatibility Report
- Age of Mythology Retold
- Steam AppID of the game: 1934680

## System Information
- GPU: 
NVIDIA Corporation AD107 [GeForce RTX 4060] (rev a)
Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. [AMD/ATI] Raphael (rev c7)

- Video driver version:  NVIDIA-SMI 580.82.09    
- Kernel version: 6.14.0-33-generic

- Proton version: All of them were tested

## I confirm:
- [ ] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [ ] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
I could play the game perfectly fine right before the new Patch. Ever since the game update I have been having compatibility issues. At first i was getting a D3D12 error, but now I cannot even start the game. 

## Reproduction

I have tried every proton version but there is no response. I have forced steam and the game to "see" only my NVIDIA gpu and did several updates but nothing has changed.

following_discussion.discussion_body: Hello @patwre, please add [`PROTON_LOG=1 %command%`](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton#runtime-config-options) to the game's launch options and attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file. (Proton logs compress well if needed.) Also, please copy the contents of Steam Runtime Diagnostics from Steam (`Steam` -> `Help` -> `Steam Runtime Diagnostics`) and put it in a [gist](https://gist.github.com/), then include a link to the gist in this issue report.

If you completely close Steam, then run `steam` from a terminal, does that have an effect? https://gist.github.com/patwre/0b750fcec20703b5f322644f6f7193ef#file-gistfile1-txt-L263-L283 and https://gist.github.com/patwre/0b750fcec20703b5f322644f6f7193ef#file-gistfile1-txt-L76 makes me suspect something similar to https://github.com/ValveSoftware/steam-for-linux/issues/8179 is happening where the AMD graphics is trying to render to the user session running on the NVIDIA proprietary driver and it's having a hard time. Also, `-dx11` in the header of the Proton log implies you have that in the game's launch options. Does removing that bring the D3D12 message back and can you collect a Proton log where you see that message?

> If you completely close Steam, then run `steam` from a terminal, does that have an effect? https://gist.github.com/patwre/0b750fcec20703b5f322644f6f7193ef#file-gistfile1-txt-L263-L283 and https://gist.github.com/patwre/0b750fcec20703b5f322644f6f7193ef#file-gistfile1-txt-L76 makes me suspect something similar to [ValveSoftware/steam-for-linux#8179](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/steam-for-linux/issues/8179) is happening where the AMD graphics is trying to render to the user session running on the NVIDIA proprietary driver and it's having a hard time. Also, `-dx11` in the header of the Proton log implies you have that in the game's launch options. Does removing that bring the D3D12 message back and can you collect a Proton log where you see that message?

[steam-1934680.log.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22977104/steam-1934680.log.gz) Here is the file where I had only the log command written the [PROTON_LOG=1 %command%]. I tried to run it from the terminal but the game won't load. It is 100% a gpu issue since the game seems to be loading when using Hotfix or even Experimental but I only see a black screen while I can hear the game music and I can blindly click stuff.

Edit: I tried to find a way through using AI because I am new to Linux and it mentioned the following
 "From the logs and your setup:

Your RTX 4060 is detected correctly by Vulkan.

Proton cannot convince the game’s internal GPU check to accept NVIDIA because it sees multiple GPUs (dual GPU hybrid setup).

This is why black screen / “unknown GPU vendor” persists no matter X11, GE-Proton9-10, or launch variables.

So, under Ubuntu 25.04 on your dual GPU system, AoM Retold is currently not playable via Proton/DX12 due to the game’s internal checks."

Is this information correct? @kisak-valve 

Edit 2: I solved the Issue by changing Auto Detect Discrete Display to "Enabled" and iGPU COnfiguration to "Disabled" From the Bios. It seemed that before that AoM would try to read the wrong GPU even though I tried to force to read the Nvidia. 
I hope this helps all future dual graphics card AoM players

> Replying to https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/8032#issuecomment-3416493423

Thankyou Thankyou Thankyou.
Disabling the iGPU on my 9800X3D + RTX 4080 Super machine in the Bios worked, tried everything else first.

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Tower!3D Pro
- Steam AppID of the game: 588190

## System Information
- GPU: RTX 3090ti
- Video driver version: 560.35.03
- Kernel version: 6.11.0-5-cachyos-lto
- Link to full system information report as Gist:  [Gist](https://gist.github.com/joni999/93970273b3810a693db63daea2acff57)
- Proton version: Proton 9.0-3

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

Proton Log:
[steam-588190.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17077892/steam-588190.log)

An important error with the speech recognition seems to be:
```
1561.239:0724:0728:trace:mscoree:wine_mono_assembly_preload_hook_v2_fn "System.Speech, Version=3.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=31bf3856ad364e35"
1561.240:0724:0728:trace:mscoree:wine_mono_assembly_preload_hook_v2_fn "System.Speech, Version=3.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=31bf3856ad364e35" #
Unhandled Exception:
System.TypeLoadException: Could not load type of field 'towerspeakbridge.Speak:synth' (1) due to: Could not load file or assembly 'System.Speech, Version=3.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=31bf3856ad364e35' or one of its dependencies.
```

## Symptoms

Speech Recognition does not work.
In this game you can use your voice to give commands to airplanes, similar to real life.
Unfortunately this does not work on Linux as it seems to use some windows components.
Also the pilots in the planes should talk back to you in synthesized/artificial voices, but this also does not work and seems to have the same root cause as the first issue.

The rest of the game works without issue.

## Reproduction

1) Start the game
2) go into options
3) Press "Test speech" - You should then hear a synthesized voiceline

To test the voice commands I would recommend to start a round and try inputting voice commands by pressing the "shift" key - After the "ding" sound you should be able to say any command into a connected microphone and it should be recognized and shown up in the top.

![Screenshot_20240920_181456](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/30b4a273-faad-4b1c-a13d-de52efdb656d)


Similar issue in the sequel: #8115

following_discussion.discussion_body: We probably just need version 3 of this assembly forwarded to version 4. But whether that actually helps will depend on the status of speech synthesis in Proton generally, and I'm not sure about that.

Committed a fix to Wine Mono for the library loading error: https://gitlab.winehq.org/mono/mono/-/commit/95d865b7bef9e90b315efb37488c3c87362dd94e

But it's not enough. Now we run into sapi.dll stubs.

I also recently attempted this on Proton Experiemental - bleeding edge, but it didn't occur to me that it was even remotely possible for the voice recognition to ever be in a working state.

Thank you for reporting, and thanks for the work on this!

With the new Proton Experimental speech synthesis patch I was thinking that maybe the voice output here would start working, but even after installing the new "Proton Voice Files" nothing changed unfortunately :(
I'm talking about the Proton Experimental change mentioned here: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/wiki/Changelog

With the new update to Proton Experimental (2025-03-20) it looks like there is a different error message now. It seems to happen for "Test recog" and "Test speech" the same.

following_discussion.discussion_body:
Hello @coreybruce, please copy your system information from Steam (`Steam` -> `Help` -> `System Information`) and put it in a [gist](https://gist.github.com/), then include a link to the gist in this issue report.

From your log:
```
err:vulkan:wine_vk_instance_load_physical_devices Failed to enumerate physical devices, res=-3
err:vulkan:__wine_create_vk_instance_with_callback Failed to load physical devices, res=-3
[...]
terminate called after throwing an instance of 'dxvk::DxvkError'
```
This hints that something went wrong with initializing Vulkan for DXVK to use.

As a test, you could try if setting the game's launch options to `PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command%` and see if that makes the game behave the same as Proton 4.11 with Proton 5.0 and newer.

> Replying to https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/4979#issuecomment-882044959

Sure here you go

https://gist.github.com/coreybruce/dcd2bec911594c9212d351ac2e7a3f98

Ok so I did it with the latest proton and it does now work but there is some wierd colour issues as if it's running in a lower colour mode

Issue was on my end and has been fixed fixing the issue. Sorry about that.
